meaning_english: Both / On both sides / Around. Example: Ambidextrous (skillful with both hands), Amphibian (living on both land and water).
War. Example: Belligerent (hostile/warlike), Antebellum (before the war).
Go / Yield / Surrender. Example: Concede (admit defeat), Recession (going backward).
Know / Aware. Example: Incognito (disguised/unknown), Cognizant (having knowledge).
Body. Example: Corporeal (relating to the body), Corporate (formed into a body/group).
Believe / Trust. Example: Credulous (too ready to believe), Credence (belief in something).
Sleep. Example: Dormant (inactive/sleeping), Dormitory (sleeping room).
Make / Do. Example: Facsimile (an exact copy/make similar), Artifact (object made by skill).
Birth / Race / Kind. Example: Genesis (origin), Progeny (offspring/birth).
Throw. Example: Conjecture (guess thrown together), Dejected (sad/thrown down).
Great / Large. Example: Magnate (powerful person), Magnanimous (great-souled/generous).
Bad / Evil. Example: Malevolent (wishing evil), Malady (bad condition/illness).
Send. Example: Remit (send back money), Emission (sending out).
Change. Example: Immutable (unchangeable), Mutagen (causes change/mutation).
New. Example: Novelty (newness), Renovate (make new again).
Peace. Example: Pacify (calm down), Appease (satisfy to make peace).
Father. Example: Paternal (fatherly), Patriarch (male head of family).
Foot. Example: Pedestrian (walker), Chiropodist (foot doctor).
Carry. Example: Import (carry in), Portfolio (case for carrying papers).
Guide / Rule. Example: Regal (royal), Rectify (make right/rule correctly).
Break / Burst. Example: Bankruptcy (broken bank), Abrupt (broken off suddenly).
Cut. Example: Bisect (cut into two), Intersection (cutting across).
Alone. Example: Soliloquy (speaking alone), Desolate (lonely/abandoned).
Look / See. Example: Retrospect (looking back), Perspicacious (keen-sighted).
Build. Example: Infrastructure (basic structure), Construe (to build meaning/interpret).
Pull / Drag. Example: Protract (pull out/lengthen), Tractable (easy to pull/manage).
Come. Example: Convene (come together), Advent (a coming/arrival).
Voice / Call. Example: Evoke (call out), Equivocal (equal voices/uncertain meaning).
Roll / Turn. Example: Evolve (roll out/develop), Revolution (turning around).
Eat / Devour. Example: Voracious (greedy eater), Carnivore (meat-eater).
